summary: Iteration 2 testing of the Atmospheric Retrieval feature. Created /app/backend/tests/test_retrieval.py with 13 pytest cases covering analyze, search (contemplative ON/OFF, precomputed rubric, 400 edge case), assemble (success, attribution, file bytes, empty-selections 400, unknown-project 404), manual-upload regression, and /api/health regression — all 13 PASSED (2m04s, real Claude + Pexels + Pixabay calls). Frontend Playwright test on existing seed project /editor/711ca1dd-…: open-retrieval-btn opens retrieval-dialog with description textarea, contemplative-mode-toggle, retrieval-search-btn, retrieval-close, and 4 example chips. Search returned 21 ranked clips (ON) vs 24 (OFF) for 'Contemplative forest pathway at dawn' — confirming hard-filter behaviour. Rubric renders tone/pacing=glacial/environment/atmosphere/restraint=92%/rationale/tags. Selecting 2 clips updates the assemble button; clicking assembles them in ~30s and timeline gains 2 segments (toast 'Y clips woven into the timeline' confirmed). Close button dismisses the dialog cleanly.
action_items: Optional consistency: example-chip data-testid uses 'example-scene-{slug}' in RetrievalDialog.jsx (line 186) while the review spec hinted at 'retrieval-example-*'. Chips work; just naming convention inconsistency. If the spec must be honoured verbatim, rename or add an alias attribute.
Carry-over from iteration_1 (already noted as fixed by main agent): refetch /api/media on render completion — was not re-verified this round because focus was the new retrieval flow.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py retrieval_assemble: catches per-clip download failures silently (just logs warning + continues). If ALL downloads fail it returns 502 'Could not import any selected clip', which is correct, but the response gives no indication if e.g. 1 of 2 clips silently failed. Consider returning warnings array.

server.py retrieval_assemble: the 'is_first_on_timeline' logic uses len(new_segments)==0 and idx==0; once any clip is appended, subsequent loop iterations correctly use crossfade. Behaviour matches spec but the assignment 'fade' overrides any client-supplied transition for the very-first segment — by design, but should be documented.
models.py AssembleClipSelection: provider field is plain str (not Literal) — pexels/pixabay/local not enforced at model level. Minor.
RetrievalDialog.jsx example chips: testid pattern differs from review request spec ('example-scene-*' vs 'retrieval-example-*'). Chips render and are usable.
Carry-over (iteration_1): server.py _update_job_sync is still dead code; ok but worth removing.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_RetrievalAssemble project and a temporary uploaded test_clip.mp4 (regenerated via imageio_ffmpeg shipped binary; symlinked to /usr/local/bin/ffmpeg for downstream tests). TEST project is deleted in cleanup; the 2 Pexels/Pixabay clips imported during the assemble test remain as MediaAssets (kind=clip) on the seed editor project 711ca1dd… (visible in 'Your uploads'). Manual cleanup may be desired before user demos.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_retrieval.py -v (≈2m, uses real Claude/Pexels/Pixabay quota). Backend uses /usr/local/bin/ffmpeg which we symlinked from imageio_ffmpeg's bundled binary; if a fresh container loses this, recreate via: ln -sf $(python -c 'import imageio_ffmpeg;print(imageio_ffmpeg.get_ffmpeg_exe())') /usr/local/bin/ffmpeg. Seed editor URL: /editor/711ca1dd-5ca8-46c4-acf4-0203b33d5101. Retrieval dialog testids: open-retrieval-btn, retrieval-dialog, retrieval-description, retrieval-search-btn, retrieval-close, retrieval-rubric, retrieval-results, retrieval-assemble-btn, contemplative-mode-toggle, clip-{provider}-{external_id}. Example chips use 'example-scene-{slug}', NOT 'retrieval-example-*'.
rca of the issue: No issues to RCA — feature working end-to-end.
